reviews.author: Paul Parent
reviews.text: Bãi Môn, located in Phú Yên Province, is a hidden gem on Vietnam’s south-central coast. Nestled beneath the iconic Mũi Đại Lãnh Lighthouse, this beach offers a peaceful escape with its wide stretch of golden sand and clear turquoise waters. Unlike the more crowded beaches of Nha Trang or Da Nang, Bãi Môn retains a sense of untouched beauty, making it perfect for travelers seeking tranquility and natural scenery. Photographers will find plenty of inspiration here: the contrast between the rolling waves, the rugged cliffs framing the bay, and the lighthouse perched above creates striking compositions, especially during sunrise when the first light of the day illuminates the horizon. The beach is also known as one of the earliest places to catch sunrise in Vietnam, adding to its unique charm. While facilities are limited, that is part of its appeal—Bãi Môn feels authentic and wild. Visitors should bring water, snacks, and sun protection, as there are only a few local vendors around. For those who love both landscape and travel photography, combining shots of the beach with the short hike up to Mũi Đại Lãnh offers a rewarding experience. Bãi Môn is not just a beach but a destination that captures the serene and raw beauty of Vietnam’s coastline. It’s a must-visit for anyone exploring Phú Yên and looking for a place to slow down, connect with nature, and capture stunning coastal views.

reviews.author: Nguyen Tuan Khoi
reviews.text: No shades, and you will have to walk through sand for a few hundred meters, but the scenery is worth it

reviews.author: Phuong
reviews.text: 2023 Pristine beauty with long white sands, clear blue waters, and majestic mountains

reviews.author: Nguyen Hanh
reviews.text: It’s such a nice beach in Vietnam still keeping an original beauty. We came there in a rainy month so we didn’t have chance to swim over there. Btw some local ppl said it’s very dangerous for swimming whole a year. Location: it’s on the right of Dai Lanh cape on the way climb down Fee: free of charge

reviews.author: Phương (Capture Charm)
reviews.text: If you're planning a trip to Phu Yen, Vietnam, there's one place you absolutely must check out: Bai Mon. Located approximately 30 kilometers south of Tuy Hoa City, Bai Mon is home to a charming lighthouse that is a must-see attraction in this region. To access Bai Mon, you'll need to purchase an entrance ticket, which costs 20,000 VND per person, and if you're arriving by car, there's a parking fee of 20,000 VND as well. Once inside, you have several options for exploration. One of the most popular activities at Bai Mon is taking a leisurely stroll up to the lighthouse to witness the spectacular sunrise. Make sure to arrive early in the morning to catch this breathtaking natural spectacle. The view from the lighthouse is simply mesmerizing, offering a panoramic vista of the surrounding coastline and the tranquil sea. For beach lovers, Bai Mon has something special to offer as well. The beach here boasts crystal-clear waters and stunning rock formations, making it an ideal spot for relaxation and photography. Be prepared to walk a bit to reach the beach, so it's a good idea to bring along an umbrella, sunscreen, and plenty of water. While at Bai Mon, consider heading to the far left corner of the beach. There, you'll find shade provided by trees, creating a perfect resting spot after a refreshing swim in the sea. This area is also great for setting up a picnic with friends and enjoying some delicious food in a tranquil setting. Bai Mon is a hidden gem in Phu Yen, offering a peaceful escape from the hustle and bustle of city life. With its pristine beach, stunning rock formations, and the iconic lighthouse, it's an excellent destination for both nature enthusiasts and those looking for a serene getaway. In conclusion, a visit to Bai Mon is a memorable experience that should be on your Phu Yen itinerary. Whether you're an early riser looking for a mesmerizing sunrise or a beach lover seeking a peaceful escape, Bai Mon has something for everyone. Don't forget to bring your sunscreen, umbrella, and plenty of water to fully enjoy this beautiful destination.

reviews.author: Vanessa King
reviews.text: I don’t recommend it except you wanna pay for a heat stroke… the beach costs entry, you are not allowed to swim or even “wade”, the light house can’t be visited from up close (and it was so hot getting up all the hill). And most frustratingly, the entire beach and landscape is laced in garbage. So.. nice views but you can get them elsewhere for free and with swimming options.

reviews.author: Thien Lam
reviews.text: Amazingly beautiful. We chose to walk down to this beach to the Lighthouse rather than follow the new path built by the local people. It took longer and more effort. But when saw the beach, we knew we made the right decision.

reviews.author: Nguyen Duc Nguyen
reviews.text: Very beautiful beach. But the wind is so strong and we are not allowed to swim.

reviews.author: Ak
reviews.text: This is a very nice beach, but it looks much nicer from the cliff near by than paying 20K per person to get into this area.

reviews.author: Bill Parfitt
reviews.text: No swimming allowed?! I pay to enter, then parking is separate payment and then no swimming allowed! They don't even bother to clean the beach - covered in plastic waste. Disappointing.

reviews.author: Piotr Chonin
reviews.text: Amazing beach. I, think it's totaly possible to camp there.

reviews.author: Daniel Hayn
reviews.text: The fee is now 30 k . Plus the additional parking fee. While this is still totally affordable, I'd say the other beaches in the area are just as beautiful as this one.

reviews.author: Michael Devery
reviews.text: It’s not a huge price at 20,000 VND per person but I think that it’s probably not worth it considering they say you can’t swim at the beach, and the lighthouse looks a lot nicer from afar than it does up close. It’s a bit of a shame beaches becoming privatised, more so for the locals. Parking for a scooter is a further 5k

reviews.author: Jean Pham
reviews.text: Awesome beach accessible by entering an entrance to a park so very few people were there. No service whatsoever, so prepare your own sunshades and water for sunbathing.

reviews.author: Vu Quang
reviews.text: The view of the ocean is very beautiful, but it can not be bathed because the waves here are very big

reviews.author: Dr. Aquarius
reviews.text: Beautiful beach but people make it dirty.

reviews.author: Hoang Nguyen Ngoc Van
reviews.text: Beautiful! The beach is like a center stage of the huge arena arround.

reviews.author: Hoàng Vu
reviews.text: A dreamy beach situated between two mountains, possessing a transparent blue water as well as a beautiful foundation of rocks. Bãi biển thơ mộng nằm giữa hai ngọn núi, vắng người tầm lúc trưa chiều với nước trong xanh và làn đá trải dài rất đẹp.

reviews.author: Ngoc Tan Huynh Tam
reviews.text: Bãi rất đẹp và hoang sơ nhưng k bơi xa bờ được nhé vì dòng chảy ra bờ rất nguy hiểm, dân địa phương cũng cảnh báo không nên tắm. Đi bộ từ bãi xe xuống, đi xuyên qua bãi cát là tới bãi môn, sau đó có thể leo bậc thang lên hải đăng Mũi điện.
